framing: Backend engineer applying to a vague startup JD with minimal requirements

jd.title: Software Engineer

requirements.text: You write clean, well-tested code and care deeply about software craft
You've shipped production software used by real users
You're comfortable working across the stack when needed
You communicate clearly in writing — we're a remote-first team

responsibilities: Build and own core platform features end to end
Work closely with our founding team to shape product direction
We don't care about your degree or years of experience. We care about what you've built and how you think.

expected_output.summary: Backend-leaning full-stack engineer with 5 years of experience building developer tools and APIs. Most recently at a YC startup shipping CLI tooling and SDK integrations used by 3K+ developers. Comfortable working across the stack, strong async communicator, and experienced in small-team environments where ownership is end-to-end.

experience.title: Software Engineer
bullets: Built and maintained CLI tooling and TypeScript SDK used by 3K+ developer accounts
Owned end-to-end feature delivery from design doc to production deployment for 8 major releases
Wrote comprehensive test suites (unit + integration) achieving 90% coverage on core modules
Authored technical blog posts and SDK documentation read by 15K+ monthly visitors

experience.title: Software Engineer
bullets: Developed internal platform features for serverless deployment infrastructure
Contributed to open-source Next.js tooling and developer experience improvements
Worked in fully remote team across 4 time zones, communicating primarily through written RFCs and async standups

experience.title: Contract Developer
bullets: Built 3 web applications for small business clients using React and Node.js

deliberate_flaws: No degree — JD says they don't care, but scorecard education check may flag
Vague JD makes it hard to prove specific requirement coverage
Short freelance stint with minimal detail
